purpose: The production pair — Qwen3-14B (the assistant, dense) + Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B (the fleet coder, MoE) — under llama.cpp b9957 on the same box/driver as the 2026-07-10 matrix and tonight's official-35B row; answers the serve-the-coder-via-llama-server question with data.

hardware.gpu: Intel Arc 140V iGPU (16GB visible, uma:1, fp16:1, KHR_coopmat)

software.llamacpp: b9957 official win-vulkan-x64 + win-sycl-x64 release binaries

methodology: llama-bench -ngl 99 -p 512 -n 128 -r 3 -o json (byte-identical protocol to docs/performance/llamacpp_vs_openvino_769_2026-07-10.json); box lean (BlarAI down, guest VM off, OVMS down); 20 s cooldown between cells; layer placement verified per model via verbose llama-server load logs (below) — both models are classic Qwen3 architectures (no DeltaNet/SSM ops, so the #19957 silent-CPU-fallback class does not apply); Vulkan device detection confirmed Arc 140V KHR_coopmat in every cell's log.

openvino_production_baseline.note: Qwen3-14B INT4 + pruned-6L draft, spec-decode ON — the production serving configuration (PERFORMANCE_LOG 2026-05-22/06-05 standing entry; 12.2 tg at 2K context). Spec-decode asymmetry named: the llama.cpp cells run WITHOUT a draft model.
OVMS 2026.2 continuous batching, INT4, 2026-06-29 (benchmark_ovms_coder-30b_2026-06-29_13-29-30.json) — the production fleet-serving configuration.

headline: OpenVINO leads llama.cpp on decode for BOTH production models on this box: 14B 13.6 (spec-decode) vs 7.52 Vulkan / 2.24 SYCL; coder-30B-A3B 38.6 (OVMS) vs 27.07 Vulkan / 6.56 SYCL. The serve-the-coder-via-llama-server trial is NOT motivated by the data. Nuance vs the 07-10 matrix: SYCL's best-decode-on-MoE result on the 35B-A3B (8.14 vs Vulkan 7.37) does NOT generalize — on the coder-30B-A3B Vulkan wins decode 4.1x (27.07 vs 6.56).
not_measured: llama.cpp with a draft model (spec-decode asymmetry vs the OV 14B baseline is named, not equalized)
prefill-vs-OV comparison for the 14B (OV baseline row cites decode; prefill baselines live in the standing prefill_14b JSONs)
quantization parity (IQ4_NL vs OV INT4 are closest analogs, not identical)
cross-day OV baselines (14B 2026-05/06, coder 2026-06-29, llama.cpp cells tonight — same box/driver family, dates named)
